food_subgroup: Fusion pizza with local Singaporean rojak-inspired toppings on a wheat-flour base

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn entry exists for Rojak Pizza specifically. FOODON_00002044 (pizza food product) is used as the closest parent category. Rojak Pizza is a Singaporean fusion variant with local rojak-inspired toppings.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC (Pizza) is used as the closest parent. No specific code exists for Singaporean fusion rojak pizza in the FoodEx2 hierarchy.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code exists for Rojak Pizza or a sufficiently close parent concept in the clinical terminology. Omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

preparation_method: topped with rojak-style sauce and ingredients
main_ingredients: rojak sauce (fermented prawn paste, tamarind, sugar)

serving.natural_language_portion: One individual Rojak Pizza weighing approximately 300 g, equivalent to a standard restaurant serving at Flavours by Saute, Funan, Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Estimated from component analysis: Pizza Margherita (USDA FoodData Central ID 1100783) and Rojak sauce/topping components (HPB Singapore Foodo, Rojak entry); weighted composite per 100 g
nutrient_source.organization: USDA FoodData Central (Foundation Foods / SR Legacy) and Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB Foodo)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Rojak Pizza is a Singaporean fusion dish not present in any single approved primary source. Nutrient values are estimated by component analysis: pizza base and cheese values derived from USDA FoodData Central pizza entries (SR Legacy); rojak sauce, pineapple, cucumber, jicama, bean sprouts, you tiao, and tofu puff values derived from HPB Singapore Foodo database entries for individual components. A weighted composite per 100 g was calculated assuming approximately 55% pizza base with cheese and 45% rojak toppings and sauce by weight. Values are best estimates and should be validated against laboratory analysis of the specific restaurant product.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of pizza estimated from analogous food: Pizza, cheese (GI = 60, glucose reference), University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Rojak Pizza. GI of 60 is adopted from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for cheese pizza (GI = 60, glucose scale), which is the closest analogous food. The rojak toppings (pineapple, jicama, cucumber, bean sprouts) are predominantly low-GI vegetables and fruits; however, the rojak sauce contains added sugar and the you tiao component is a fried dough product, which may slightly elevate the overall GI relative to plain cheese pizza. GI = 60 is therefore considered a defensible central estimate. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (60 × 85.5) / 100 = 51.3, where carbohydrate per 300 g serving = 28.5 × 3 = 85.5 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~480 mg per 100 g; ~1440 mg per serving) from rojak prawn paste sauce, cheese, and processed dough
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit Rojak Pizza to occasional consumption and request reduced-sauce portions, as a single serving may provide approximately 60–70% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium glycemic index (GI 60) combined with a large carbohydrate load per serving (~85.5 g carbohydrate per 300 g serving) produces a high glycemic load (~51), posing significant postprandial glucose risk
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid or strictly limit Rojak Pizza due to its high glycemic load per serving; if consumed, portion size should be reduced to one or two slices and paired with non-starchy vegetables to blunt the postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate saturated fat (~3.9 g per 100 g) from cheese and fried you tiao, combined with high sodium, contributes to cardiovascular risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should treat Rojak Pizza as an occasional indulgence, prioritising smaller portions and requesting less cheese and sauce to reduce saturated fat and sodium intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Rojak Pizza is a Singaporean fusion dish that combines the format of a Western-style baked pizza with the bold, pungent flavours of traditional Singaporean rojak. The pizza base is a standard wheat-flour dough, baked until crisp, and topped with mozzarella cheese. Instead of conventional pizza sauce, a rojak-style sauce made from fermented prawn paste (hae ko), tamarind, palm sugar, and chilli is spread over the base, delivering the characteristic sweet-sour-pungent profile of Singaporean rojak. Toppings typically include fresh pineapple chunks, cucumber slices, jicama (bangkuang), bean sprouts, crispy you tiao (fried dough fritters), and tofu puffs, all finished with crushed roasted peanuts and sesame seeds. The result is a dish with contrasting textures — chewy dough, melted cheese, crunchy peanuts, and soft fruit — and a complex flavour profile that is simultaneously sweet, sour, savoury, and umami-rich. Rojak Pizza is a creative restaurant offering found in Singapore's casual dining scene, exemplified by establishments such as Flavours by Saute at Funan Mall. Nutritionally, it is a calorie-dense, carbohydrate-rich dish with moderate protein from cheese and tofu, and notable sodium from the prawn paste sauce. Its medium GI (estimated ~60) combined with a large carbohydrate load per serving makes it a high-glycemic-load meal, requiring caution for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or hypertension. The dish is not suitable for vegetarians, vegans, or those with shellfish, peanut, gluten, dairy, or soy allergies.

confidence_reason: Rojak Pizza is a proprietary fusion dish served by a single restaurant (Flavours by Saute, Funan, Singapore) and is not present in any approved primary nutrient database. Nutrient values are estimated by component analysis using USDA FoodData Central and HPB Singapore Foodo data for individual ingredients, with a weighted composite calculation. GI is estimated from the closest analogous food (cheese pizza, GI 60) in the University of Sydney GI Database. All values should be treated as best estimates pending laboratory analysis of the specific restaurant product.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated by component analysis (55% pizza base with cheese, 45% rojak toppings and sauce by weight) using USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy pizza entries and HPB Singapore Foodo individual component entries. These are best estimates and carry inherent uncertainty. 2. GI is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for cheese pizza (GI = 60); no direct GI measurement for Rojak Pizza exists in any approved source. 3. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the component sources used for estimation. 4. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the component sources used for estimation. 5. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the component sources used for estimation. 6. Halal status is uncertain: traditional rojak sauce contains hae ko (fermented prawn paste), which may not be halal-certified depending on the restaurant's sourcing. 7. The dish contains multiple major allergens: gluten, dairy, peanuts, shellfish (prawn paste), sesame, and soy. 8. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (60 × 85.5) / 100 = 51.3, where carbohydrate per 300 g serving = 85.5 g. 9. This record should be flagged for laboratory validation if incorporated into a production nutrition database.
